description: The rose is a woody perennial flowering plant known for its beauty, fragrance, and cultural significance. With over 300 species and thousands of cultivars, roses are one of the most popular and versatile flowers in the world.
water: Moderate — water deeply 2-3 times per week

benefits.description: Rich in antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and polyphenols
Used in herbal teas to aid digestion and reduce inflammation
Rose water helps soothe skin irritation and acts as a natural astringent
May help reduce stress and anxiety through aromatherapy
Aromatherapy with rose oil promotes relaxation and emotional balance
Rose hip tea boosts immune system with high vitamin C content
Natural mood enhancement and stress reduction
Rose-infused skincare products help hydrate and rejuvenate skin
Luxury perfumes — rose is the most widely used floral fragrance note
Essential oils — rose otto and rose absolute are highly prized
Body sprays, lotions, and scented candles
Aromatherapy oils for relaxation and mood enhancement
Gardens — a classic choice for formal and cottage gardens
Weddings — the most popular flower for bridal bouquets
Home decoration — cut roses brighten any room
Landscaping — hedges, trellises, and garden borders
Rose water used in Middle Eastern and Indian cuisine
Rose petals candied for cake decorations and desserts
Rose hip jam and jelly are rich in flavor and nutrients

care_tips.title: Plant in full sun
care_tips.description: Roses need at least 6 hours of direct sunlight daily for optimal blooming.

care_tips.title: Water at the base
care_tips.description: Avoid wetting foliage to prevent fungal diseases. Water deeply at the root zone.

care_tips.title: Prune annually
care_tips.description: Prune in early spring before new growth. Remove dead, diseased, or crossing branches.

care_tips.title: Fertilize regularly
care_tips.description: Feed with a balanced rose fertilizer every 4-6 weeks during the growing season.

care_tips.title: Mulch to retain moisture
care_tips.description: Apply 2-3 inches of organic mulch around the base to conserve water and suppress weeds.

care_tips.title: Monitor for pests
care_tips.description: Watch for aphids, black spot, and powdery mildew. Treat early with neem oil or insecticidal soap.

description: Lavender is a fragrant herb prized for its calming aroma, beautiful purple flowers, and versatile uses in medicine, perfumery, and cooking. It thrives in sunny, well-drained conditions.
soil: Well-drained, sandy or gravelly soil, pH 6.5-7.5

benefits.description: Promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality
Natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties
Helps relieve headaches and migraines when applied topically
Aids digestion and reduces bloating when consumed as tea
Reduces anxiety and stress through aromatherapy
Improves sleep quality and helps with insomnia
Natural insect repellent
Classic fragrance note in soaps, lotions, and perfumes
Lavender essential oil is one of the most popular aromatherapy oils
Used in sachets, potpourri, and linen sprays
Beautiful purple flower spikes in summer gardens
Excellent for borders, hedges, and container gardens
Dried lavender used in floral arrangements and crafts
Lavender honey is a gourmet delicacy
Used in baked goods like lavender cookies and cakes
Lavender-infused teas and lemonades

care_tips.title: Plant in full sun
care_tips.description: Lavender needs at least 6-8 hours of direct sunlight daily.

care_tips.title: Ensure excellent drainage
care_tips.description: Lavender hates wet feet. Plant in raised beds or add gravel to heavy soil.

care_tips.title: Water sparingly
care_tips.description: Allow soil to dry between waterings. Overwatering is the most common cause of death.

care_tips.title: Prune after flowering
care_tips.description: Cut back stems by one-third after blooming to maintain shape and encourage new growth.

care_tips.title: Mulch with gravel
care_tips.description: Use gravel or pebble mulch rather than bark to keep moisture away from the crown.

description: Jasmine is celebrated for its intensely fragrant white flowers and twining vines. It is one of the most important flowers in perfumery and has deep cultural significance across Asia.

benefits.description: Used in traditional medicine as an antidepressant and relaxant
Jasmine tea is rich in antioxidants and may boost immunity
Anti-inflammatory properties help with muscle and joint pain
Jasmine aromatherapy reduces anxiety and improves mood
Jasmine tea promotes cardiovascular health
Natural aphrodisiac properties
Essential ingredient in luxury perfumes and colognes
Jasmine absolute is one of the most expensive fragrance materials
Used in scented oils, incense, and body products
Beautiful climbing vine for trellises, walls, and arbors
Fragrant night-blooming flowers ideal for patio and entryway gardens
Used in Hindu and Buddhist ceremonies and offerings
Symbol of purity and grace in many Asian cultures
Jasmine flowers used to scent tea, particularly green tea
Jasmine syrup used in desserts and cocktails

care_tips.title: Provide support for climbing
care_tips.description: Jasmine needs a trellis, arbor, or fence to climb. Train vines gently.

care_tips.title: Water consistently
care_tips.description: Keep soil moist but not waterlogged. Reduce watering in winter.

care_tips.title: Fertilize monthly
care_tips.description: Feed with a balanced fertilizer during the growing season for abundant blooms.

care_tips.title: Prune after flowering
care_tips.description: Cut back spent flowers and wayward vines to maintain shape and encourage reblooming.

care_tips.title: Protect from frost
care_tips.description: In colder climates, grow in containers and move indoors during winter.

description: Hibiscus produces large, vibrant trumpet-shaped flowers in a stunning array of colors. It is beloved for its tropical aesthetic and multiple practical uses in medicine and cuisine.
water: Moderate to High — keep soil moist

benefits.description: Hibiscus tea may help lower blood pressure and cholesterol
Rich in vitamin C and antioxidants
Traditional remedy for colds and respiratory issues
Anti-inflammatory properties aid skin health
Supports liver health and aids digestion
Hydrating and refreshing beverage
Hibiscus extract used in tropical fragrances
Hair oils and skincare products featuring hibiscus
Show-stopping tropical garden centerpiece
Container plant for patios and balconies
Popular in Hawaiian and tropical-themed events
Dried hibiscus flowers used in teas (agua de jamaica)
Hibiscus syrup for cocktails and desserts
Edible flowers used as garnish in salads

care_tips.title: Provide ample sunlight
care_tips.description: Hibiscus needs at least 6 hours of direct sun for best blooming.

care_tips.title: Water frequently
care_tips.description: Keep soil consistently moist, especially during hot weather. Never let it dry out completely.

care_tips.title: Fertilize weekly
care_tips.description: Use a high-potassium fertilizer weekly during the growing season for continuous blooms.

care_tips.title: Prune to shape
care_tips.description: Cut back in early spring to encourage bushier growth and more flowers.

care_tips.title: Watch for pests
care_tips.description: Aphids, whiteflies, and spider mites can be problematic. Treat with neem oil.

description: Chamomile is a gentle, daisy-like herb famous for its calming tea and apple-like fragrance. It is one of the oldest documented medicinal plants used by humans.

benefits.description: Promotes relaxation and helps treat insomnia
Anti-inflammatory properties aid digestive issues
Soothes skin irritations and minor wounds
May help reduce menstrual cramps and muscle spasms
Calming tea is ideal for bedtime routines
Supports immune system with mild antibacterial properties
Chamomile essential oil used in aromatherapy blends
Apple-like scent used in natural hair and skincare products
Delicate daisy-like flowers add charm to herb gardens
Most popular herbal tea worldwide
Edible flowers used as garnish in salads and desserts

care_tips.title: Sow seeds directly
care_tips.description: Chamomile grows easily from seed scattered directly in the garden after frost.

care_tips.title: Water moderately
care_tips.description: Water regularly until established, then reduce. Chamomile is somewhat drought-tolerant.

care_tips.title: Harvest regularly
care_tips.description: Pick flowers when petals begin to fold back for the best tea flavor.

care_tips.title: Self-seeds readily
care_tips.description: Allow some flowers to go to seed for volunteers next season.

description: The Areca Palm, also known as the Butterfly Palm, is one of the most popular indoor palms. Its feathery, arching fronds bring a tropical feel to any space and it is renowned for its air-purifying qualities.

benefits.description: Elegant indoor plant that adds tropical ambiance
Popular in offices, hotels, and commercial spaces
Natural air purifier — removes formaldehyde and benzene
Excellent for tropical-themed garden designs
Works well as a privacy screen or hedge in warm climates
Ideal for poolside and patio container planting

care_tips.title: Provide bright indirect light
care_tips.description: Place near a window with filtered light. Direct sun can scorch the fronds.

care_tips.title: Keep soil consistently moist
care_tips.description: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Reduce watering in winter.

care_tips.title: Mist regularly
care_tips.description: Areca palms love humidity. Mist the fronds daily or use a humidifier.

care_tips.title: Fertilize seasonally
care_tips.description: Feed with a balanced liquid fertilizer monthly during spring and summer.

care_tips.title: Remove yellow fronds
care_tips.description: Trim brown or yellow fronds at the base to keep the plant looking tidy.

description: Aloe Vera is a succulent plant species renowned for its medicinal gel-filled leaves. It is one of the most widely used medicinal plants in the world, valued for both its healing properties and ornamental appeal.

benefits.description: Heals burns, cuts, and skin irritations with cooling gel
Anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties
Aloe juice aids digestion and supports gut health
Rich in vitamins A, C, E, and B12
Hydrates and rejuvenates skin naturally
Boosts immune system with antioxidants
Modern sculptural appearance suits contemporary decor
Low-maintenance indoor plant ideal for beginners

care_tips.title: Water infrequently
care_tips.description: Allow soil to dry completely between waterings. Overwatering causes root rot.

care_tips.title: Use well-draining soil
care_tips.description: Cactus or succulent mix is essential. Add perlite or sand to improve drainage.

care_tips.title: Provide bright light
care_tips.description: Aloe needs several hours of bright, indirect light. A south or west-facing window is ideal.

care_tips.title: Keep at room temperature
care_tips.description: Aloe thrives between 55-80°F. Protect from frost.

care_tips.title: Harvest leaves carefully
care_tips.description: Cut outer leaves at the base when needed. Leave inner young leaves to grow.

description: The Coconut Palm is one of the most useful trees in the world, providing food, water, oil, fiber, and timber. Its graceful, leaning trunk and feathery fronds are iconic symbols of tropical beaches.

benefits.description: Iconic tropical landscape tree for coastal and resort settings
Creates dramatic silhouette in beachfront and tropical gardens
Excellent for creating shade in tropical landscapes
Coconut water — natural electrolyte-rich beverage
Coconut meat, milk, and oil used in cuisines worldwide
Coconut oil has antimicrobial and moisturizing properties
Coconut water is a natural source of electrolytes and minerals

care_tips.title: Plant in full tropical sun
care_tips.description: Coconut palms need direct sun for at least 6 hours daily.

care_tips.title: Provide sandy well-drained soil
care_tips.description: They tolerate salty coastal conditions but need good drainage.

care_tips.title: Water regularly when young
care_tips.description: Young palms need consistent moisture. Mature trees are drought-tolerant.

care_tips.title: Fertilize with palm-specific food
care_tips.description: Use a fertilizer formulated for palms with micronutrients like magnesium and manganese.

care_tips.title: Protect from cold
care_tips.description: Coconut palms are tropical and cannot tolerate frost.

description: The sunflower is a towering annual plant famous for its large, cheerful yellow flower heads that track the sun. It is widely cultivated for its seeds, oil, and ornamental value across the globe.
water: Moderate — water deeply once a week

benefits.description: Sunflower seeds are rich in vitamin E and selenium
Sunflower oil contains heart-healthy unsaturated fats
Sunflower seeds support heart health and reduce inflammation
Cheerful appearance boosts mood and mental wellbeing
Sunflower extract used in mild floral fragrances
Sunflower oil is a base carrier for essential oil blends
Dramatic tall flowers create garden focal points
Popular in cut flower arrangements and rustic bouquets
Children's gardens — fast-growing and rewarding
Roasted sunflower seeds are a nutritious snack
Sunflower oil is used for cooking and baking

care_tips.title: Plant in full sun
care_tips.description: Sunflowers need 6-8 hours of direct sunlight daily. They literally grow toward the sun.

care_tips.title: Water deeply but infrequently
care_tips.description: Water once a week with deep soaking to encourage deep root growth.

care_tips.title: Stake tall varieties
care_tips.description: Tall sunflower varieties may need staking to prevent toppling in wind.

care_tips.title: Protect from birds
care_tips.description: Cover seed heads with netting if you want to harvest seeds before birds do.

care_tips.title: Harvest at the right time
care_tips.description: Harvest seeds when the back of the flower head turns brown and seeds are plump.

description: Tulips are spring-blooming bulbs prized for their cup-shaped flowers in virtually every color. They sparked economic mania in the Dutch Golden Age and remain one of the most popular garden flowers worldwide.
water: Moderate — keep soil moist during growth

benefits.description: Tulip extracts have been used in traditional skincare
Gardening with tulips provides physical activity and stress relief
Some tulip varieties have a subtle, sweet fragrance
Tulip-inspired synthetic notes used in modern perfumery
Iconic spring garden flower with endless color variety
Excellent for borders, containers, and mass plantings
One of the most popular cut flowers worldwide
Tulip petals are edible and can be used as garnish

care_tips.title: Plant bulbs in fall
care_tips.description: Plant tulip bulbs 6-8 inches deep in well-drained soil before the first frost.

care_tips.title: Water after planting
care_tips.description: Water bulbs thoroughly after planting, then leave them alone over winter.

care_tips.title: Fertilize in spring
care_tips.description: Apply a balanced bulb fertilizer when shoots first emerge in spring.

care_tips.title: Deadhead after blooming
care_tips.description: Remove spent flowers but leave foliage to die back naturally to nourish the bulb.

care_tips.title: Lift and store bulbs
care_tips.description: In warm climates, lift bulbs after foliage dies and store cool and dry for replanting in fall.

description: Orchids are one of the largest and most diverse plant families on Earth. Moth orchids (Phalaenopsis) are the most popular houseplant orchids, valued for their long-lasting exotic blooms.
soil: Orchid bark mix (not regular potting soil)

benefits.description: Some orchid species used in traditional Chinese medicine
Orchid-growing is a meditative hobby that reduces stress
Improves indoor air quality
Vanilla orchid produces vanilla beans used in perfumery
Many orchids have exquisite, complex fragrances
Exotic long-lasting blooms — flowers last 2-4 months
Elegant indoor plant for modern home decor
Popular gift plant for special occasions
Vanilla beans from vanilla orchids are used in baking

care_tips.title: Use orchid bark mix
care_tips.description: Never use regular potting soil. Orchids need bark, sphagnum moss, or perlite for root aeration.

care_tips.title: Water sparingly
care_tips.description: Water once every 7-14 days when the bark is nearly dry. Overwatering is the #1 killer.

care_tips.title: Provide bright indirect light
care_tips.description: Place near an east or west window. Direct sun burns leaves, low light prevents blooming.

care_tips.title: Maintain humidity
care_tips.description: Orchids love 50-70% humidity. Use a humidity tray or mist occasionally.

care_tips.title: Fertilize weakly weekly
care_tips.description: Feed with orchid fertilizer at half strength every week during the growing season.

care_tips.title: Repot every 1-2 years
care_tips.description: Repot when bark breaks down or roots outgrow the pot. Use fresh bark mix.

description: Lilies are tall, stately perennials with large, showy, often fragrant flowers. They have deep cultural symbolism representing purity, renewal, and majesty across many civilizations.
water: Moderate — keep soil moist but not waterlogged

benefits.description: Lily extracts used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory properties
Lily root used in some Chinese herbal remedies
Fragrant lilies promote relaxation and stress relief
Intoxicating fragrance used in luxury perfumes and candles
Lily of the valley is a classic perfume note
Majestic garden flowers with spectacular trumpet-shaped blooms
Excellent cut flowers with long vase life
Symbol of Easter and spring celebrations
Symbol of purity in Christian iconography, particularly the Madonna lily
Used in Easter and spring religious ceremonies

care_tips.title: Plant bulbs in spring or fall
care_tips.description: Plant lily bulbs 4-6 inches deep in well-drained soil with the pointed end up.

care_tips.title: Provide good drainage
care_tips.description: Lilies rot in wet soil. Raised beds or sloping sites are ideal.

care_tips.title: Mulch to keep roots cool
care_tips.description: Apply 2-3 inches of mulch around the base to keep roots cool in summer.

care_tips.title: Stake tall varieties
care_tips.description: Tall lily varieties may need staking to keep heavy flower heads upright.

care_tips.title: Remove spent flowers
care_tips.description: Deadhead after blooming but leave stems to die back naturally for next year's growth.

description: Marigolds are cheerful, sun-loving annuals with bright orange and yellow flowers. They are beloved in gardens for their pest-repelling properties and are deeply woven into cultural celebrations.

benefits.description: Marigold (calendula) ointment heals wounds and skin irritations
Anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties
Used in natural skincare and diaper rash creams
Natural pest repellent in companion planting
Edible flowers add color and nutrients to salads
Vibrant color from spring until frost
Excellent for borders, containers, and mass plantings
Essential in Day of the Dead celebrations (cempasúchil)
Central to Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead) altars and decorations
Used in Hindu ceremonies and temple offerings
Edible petals used as a saffron substitute in cooking

care_tips.title: Plant in full sun
care_tips.description: Marigolds need at least 6 hours of direct sun for best blooming.

care_tips.title: Deadhead spent blooms
care_tips.description: Remove faded flowers to encourage continuous blooming all season.

care_tips.title: Water at the base
care_tips.description: Avoid wetting foliage to prevent fungal issues. Water at soil level.

care_tips.title: Fertilize lightly
care_tips.description: Marigolds need little fertilizer. Too much nitrogen produces leaves instead of flowers.

care_tips.title: Use as companion plants
care_tips.description: Plant near vegetables to repel nematodes, aphids, and other pests naturally.

description: Peonies are beloved perennials with enormous, lush, fragrant blooms in shades of pink, white, and red. They are long-lived plants that can thrive for decades, making them treasured garden heirlooms.

benefits.description: Peony root used in traditional Chinese medicine (Bai Shao) for pain relief
Anti-inflammatory properties used to treat arthritis and muscle cramps
Growing peonies provides rewarding gardening experience and stress relief
Luxurious floral scent used in high-end perfumes
Peony is a popular note in candles and home fragrances
Spectacular large blooms — up to 10 inches in diameter
Favorite cut flower for weddings and special occasions
Long-lived perennial that improves with age
Peony petals can be used to make tea and floral water

care_tips.title: Plant in fall
care_tips.description: Plant peony roots (bare root) in early fall. Set the eyes (buds) 1-2 inches below soil surface.

care_tips.title: Provide full sun
care_tips.description: Peonies need at least 6 hours of sun for best flowering. Too much shade reduces blooms.

care_tips.title: Support heavy blooms
care_tips.description: Use peony rings or stakes to keep heavy flower heads from drooping to the ground.

care_tips.title: Water during dry spells
care_tips.description: Water deeply during extended dry periods, especially when buds are forming.

care_tips.title: Cut back in fall
care_tips.description: Cut stems to ground level after frost kills the foliage. Remove debris to prevent disease.

care_tips.title: Be patient first year
care_tips.description: Peonies often don't bloom well the first year. They need time to establish.

description: The lotus is an aquatic perennial with stunning pink or white flowers that rise above the water. It holds profound spiritual symbolism in Eastern religions and is renowned for its remarkable seed longevity.
soil: Heavy clay loam in aquatic containers

benefits.description: All parts of lotus used in traditional Asian medicine
Lotus seeds are rich in protein, fiber, and antioxidants
Lotus root is used to treat digestive issues
Lotus tea promotes relaxation and mental clarity
Low-calorie lotus root is rich in dietary fiber
Stunning aquatic centerpiece for ponds and water gardens
Iconic subject in Asian art and photography
Sacred symbol in Buddhism representing purity and enlightenment
Important in Hinduism — associated with Lakshmi and Brahma
Lotus root is a popular vegetable in Asian cuisine
Lotus seeds are eaten raw, roasted, or used in soups and desserts
Lotus tea is made from dried lotus leaves

care_tips.title: Grow in aquatic containers
care_tips.description: Plant lotus in a wide, shallow container with heavy clay soil. Submerge in 2-6 inches of water.

care_tips.title: Provide full sun
care_tips.description: Lotus needs at least 6 hours of direct sun daily. No blooms in shade.

care_tips.title: Use warm water
care_tips.description: Lotus thrives in warm water above 70°F. Cold water slows growth significantly.

care_tips.title: Fertilize with aquatic tabs
care_tips.description: Use slow-release aquatic fertilizer tablets pushed into the soil monthly.

care_tips.title: Protect in winter
care_tips.description: In cold climates, move containers to a frost-free location or lower to the deepest part of the pond.

description: Cherry blossoms, or sakura, are the fleeting flowers of ornamental cherry trees. They are revered in Japan as symbols of the transient beauty of life and draw millions of visitors during spring bloom season.

benefits.description: Cherry blossom extract used in skincare for anti-aging benefits
Sakura tea is rich in antioxidants
Hanami (flower viewing) promotes mental wellbeing and mindfulness
Cherry blossoms contain coumaric acid with anti-inflammatory properties
Delicate floral fragrance used in spring-themed perfumes
Sakura-scented body care products are hugely popular in Asia
World-famous spring bloom — iconic landscape trees
Spectacular pink cloud-like displays along boulevards and parks
Cherry blossom festivals are major cultural events worldwide
Symbol of impermanence in Japanese Buddhism
Central to hanami tradition dating back over 1,000 years
Sakura-flavored sweets, lattes, and mochi
Pickled cherry blossoms (sakura-zuke) used in traditional wagashi

care_tips.title: Plant in spring or fall
care_tips.description: Plant cherry trees in well-drained soil with the graft union above ground level.

care_tips.title: Prune after flowering
care_tips.description: Prune cherry trees immediately after blooming to shape and remove dead branches.

care_tips.title: Water deeply during dry spells
care_tips.description: Water deeply once a week during dry periods. Avoid overhead watering to prevent disease.

care_tips.title: Fertilize in early spring
care_tips.description: Apply a balanced fertilizer in early spring before new growth emerges.

care_tips.title: Protect from pests
care_tips.description: Watch for aphids, caterpillars, and fungal diseases. Treat promptly.

description: Frangipani, or plumeria, is a tropical tree with intensely fragrant, waxy flowers in white, pink, yellow, and red. It is strongly associated with Hawaiian leis and tropical paradise aesthetics.

benefits.description: Plumeria extracts used in traditional medicine for anti-inflammatory purposes
Frangipani aromatherapy promotes relaxation and stress relief
Intensely fragrant — a key note in tropical perfumes
Plumeria essential oil used in aromatherapy and scented products
Hawaiian leis are traditionally made from frangipani flowers
Tropical beauty with stunning, long-lasting flowers
Perfect specimen tree for tropical and Mediterranean gardens
Iconic Hawaiian lei flower
Used in temple offerings in Bali and Southeast Asia
Planted in Buddhist temple gardens for its beauty and fragrance

care_tips.title: Provide full sun
care_tips.description: Plumeria needs at least 6 hours of direct sun for abundant flowering.

care_tips.title: Use well-draining soil
care_tips.description: Cactus or succulent mix works well. Plumerias are susceptible to root rot in heavy soil.

care_tips.title: Water sparingly in winter
care_tips.description: Reduce watering during dormancy. Resume when new leaves appear in spring.

care_tips.title: Fertilize with high phosphorus
care_tips.description: Use a fertilizer high in phosphorus (middle number) to promote blooming.

care_tips.title: Protect from frost
care_tips.description: In cold climates, grow in containers and move indoors when temperatures drop below 50°F.

description: Gardenias are prized for their creamy white, waxy flowers with an intoxicating sweet fragrance. They are considered one of the most fragrant flowers in the world but can be challenging to grow.

benefits.description: Gardenia fruit used in traditional Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties
Gardenia extracts used to treat fever and swelling
Aromatherapy with gardenia promotes calm and reduces anxiety
One of the most beloved floral fragrance notes in luxury perfumery
Gardenia absolute is a prized ingredient in high-end fragrances
Scented candles, soaps, and lotions featuring gardenia
Elegant white flowers with a stunning fragrance
Beautiful container plant for patios and entryways
Popular in bridal bouquets and corsages

care_tips.title: Maintain acidic soil
care_tips.description: Gardenias need acidic soil (pH 5.0-6.5). Use fertilizer formulated for acid-loving plants.

care_tips.title: Provide consistent moisture
care_tips.description: Keep soil evenly moist but not waterlogged. Fluctuations cause bud drop.

care_tips.title: Increase humidity
care_tips.description: Gardenias need high humidity. Mist daily or place on a humidity tray.

care_tips.title: Provide bright indirect light
care_tips.description: Morning sun with afternoon shade is ideal. Too much direct sun scorches leaves.

care_tips.title: Prune after flowering
care_tips.description: Prune to shape after blooming ends. Avoid heavy pruning which reduces next year's flowers.

description: The dandelion is one of the most recognizable and useful wild plants. Often dismissed as a weed, it is actually a nutrient-dense edible plant with a long history of medicinal use across cultures.

benefits.description: Dandelion root is a powerful liver detoxifier and diuretic
Rich in vitamins A, C, K, and minerals like iron and calcium
Dandelion tea aids digestion and reduces bloating
Traditional remedy for skin conditions like eczema
Supports liver function and natural detoxification
Dandelion root coffee is a healthy caffeine-free alternative
Prebiotic fiber supports gut health
Bright yellow flowers signal the arrival of spring
Whimsical seed heads (blowballs) beloved by children
Young dandelion leaves make nutritious salad greens
Dandelion flowers can be made into wine, jelly, and syrup
Roasted dandelion root is a popular coffee substitute

care_tips.title: Grows anywhere
care_tips.description: Dandelions need virtually no care. They thrive in lawns, fields, cracks in pavement — anywhere.

care_tips.title: Harvest young leaves
care_tips.description: Pick leaves in early spring before flowers appear for the best flavor (less bitter).

care_tips.title: Harvest roots in fall
care_tips.description: Dig roots in autumn of the second year for the best medicinal potency.

care_tips.title: Use as a lawn indicator
care_tips.description: Dandelions indicate compacted soil. Their deep taproots actually help break up soil.

description: Eucalyptus is a fast-growing evergreen tree with distinctive blue-green, aromatic leaves. It is one of the most important sources of essential oils and is widely used in medicine, perfumery, and floristry.

benefits.description: Eucalyptus oil is a powerful decongestant and respiratory aid
Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties
Used in chest rubs, inhalants, and cough drops
Natural pain reliever for muscle and joint pain
Clears nasal congestion and supports respiratory health
Natural insect repellent
Aromatherapy promotes clear breathing and mental alertness
Fresh, camphoraceous scent used in colognes and body products
Eucalyptus essential oil is a staple in aromatherapy blends
Popular in shower steamers, soaps, and room sprays
Beautiful silvery-blue foliage used in floral arrangements
Aromatic foliage adds sensory interest to gardens
Dramatic, fast-growing specimen tree

care_tips.title: Plant in full sun
care_tips.description: Eucalyptus needs full sun and plenty of space. Do not plant near buildings or pipes.

care_tips.title: Water deeply but infrequently
care_tips.description: Water deeply once established. Eucalyptus is very drought-tolerant.

care_tips.title: Prune to control size
care_tips.description: Coppicing (cutting to the ground) keeps eucalyptus shrub-sized. Otherwise it grows into a large tree.

care_tips.title: Fertilize lightly
care_tips.description: Eucalyptus needs little fertilizer. A light application in spring is sufficient.

care_tips.title: Harvest leaves for oil
care_tips.description: Crush leaves to release aromatic oil. Steam distillation produces concentrated essential oil.

description: Snake plant, also called mother-in-law's tongue, is one of the most popular and resilient houseplants. Its striking upright sword-like leaves with yellow margins make it a favorite for modern interiors.

benefits.description: Snake plant purifies air by removing toxins like formaldehyde and benzene
Converts CO2 to oxygen at night — ideal for bedrooms
NASA-approved air purifying plant
Increases humidity and reduces airborne allergens
Striking architectural foliage for modern interiors
Nearly unkillable — perfect for beginners and offices
Available in many varieties with different leaf patterns

care_tips.title: Water very sparingly
care_tips.description: Snake plants are succulents. Water every 2-6 weeks depending on light. Overwatering kills them.

care_tips.title: Tolerates low light
care_tips.description: Snake plants thrive in low to bright indirect light. They adapt to almost any indoor condition.

care_tips.title: Use well-draining soil
care_tips.description: Cactus or succulent mix is essential. Add perlite to regular potting soil if needed.

care_tips.title: Keep above 50°F
care_tips.description: Snake plants are tropical and cannot tolerate cold temperatures or frost.

care_tips.title: Propagate by division
care_tips.description: Divide overcrowded plants by separating rhizomes. Each section needs roots and a leaf.

description: Peace lily is a popular flowering houseplant known for its elegant white spathes (flowers) and glossy green leaves. It is one of the best air-purifying plants and adapts well to indoor conditions.

benefits.description: Peace lily improves indoor air quality by removing VOCs
Increases humidity and reduces airborne mold spores
Peace lily indicates when it needs water — leaves droop dramatically then recover
NASA-approved for removing benzene, formaldehyde, and trichloroethylene
Elegant white flowers bloom multiple times per year
Lush tropical foliage — one of the most popular houseplants
Excellent gift plant for housewarmings and sympathy

care_tips.title: Keep soil consistently moist
care_tips.description: Water when the top inch of soil feels dry. Peace lilies wilt dramatically when thirsty.

care_tips.title: Provide indirect light
care_tips.description: Peace lilies thrive in medium to low indirect light. Direct sun burns leaves.

care_tips.title: Mist regularly
care_tips.description: Peace lilies love humidity. Mist leaves weekly or place on a humidity tray.

care_tips.title: Fertilize monthly
care_tips.description: Feed with balanced houseplant fertilizer monthly during spring and summer.

care_tips.title: Wipe leaves clean
care_tips.description: Dust collects on large leaves. Wipe with a damp cloth to keep them photosynthesizing efficiently.

description: Bougainvillea is a vibrant, thorny vine renowned for its spectacular displays of papery bracts in vivid shades of magenta, purple, orange, and white. It thrives in hot, dry climates and is a staple of Mediterranean and tropical gardens.

benefits.description: Bougainvillea extracts used in traditional medicine for cough and respiratory issues
Dense thorny growth makes excellent natural security hedge
Explosive color in hot, dry climates where few plants thrive
Excellent for trellises, walls, and cascading over fences
Thrives in coastal gardens with salt spray
Creates dramatic vertical interest with vibrant color
Ideal for xeriscaping and drought-tolerant gardens

care_tips.title: Provide full sun
care_tips.description: Bougainvillea needs at least 6 hours of direct sun daily. Less sun means fewer blooms.

care_tips.title: Water sparingly
care_tips.description: Bougainvillea blooms best when stressed. Water only when soil is dry to the touch.

care_tips.title: Prune after bloom cycle
care_tips.description: Prune after each bloom cycle to shape and encourage new growth (which produces flowers).

care_tips.title: Fertilize for blooms
care_tips.description: Use a fertilizer high in phosphorus and potassium. Avoid high nitrogen which produces leaves.

care_tips.title: Protect from frost
care_tips.description: Bougainvillea is frost-tender. In cold climates, grow in containers and overwinter indoors.

description: Dahlias are tuberous perennials with an astonishing range of flower forms, sizes, and colors. From tiny pompons to giant dinner-plate blooms, dahlias are a favorite of flower arrangers and gardeners.

benefits.description: Dahlia tubers were used by Aztecs as a food source and medicine
Flower arranging with dahlias provides creative therapy and stress relief
Growing dahlias encourages outdoor activity
Incredible variety — from 1-inch pompons to 12-inch dinner-plate blooms
Excellent cut flower with long vase life
Blooms from midsummer until first frost
Dahlia tubers are edible and were cultivated as food by the Aztecs

care_tips.title: Plant tubers in spring
care_tips.description: Plant dahlia tubers after all danger of frost has passed. Position eyes facing up, 4-6 inches deep.

care_tips.title: Pinch for bushier growth
care_tips.description: Pinch out the center tip when plants are 12 inches tall to encourage branching and more flowers.

care_tips.title: Water consistently
care_tips.description: Water deeply 2-3 times per week. Consistent moisture is key for large blooms.

care_tips.title: Fertilize regularly
care_tips.description: Feed with a low-nitrogen fertilizer every 2-4 weeks throughout the growing season.

care_tips.title: Lift tubers in fall
care_tips.description: In cold climates, dig up tubers after first frost, dry them, and store in a cool, dark place over winter.

description: Ferns are ancient, non-flowering plants with delicate, feathery fronds. They have thrived on Earth for over 360 million years and are prized in gardens and homes for their lush, textural foliage.

benefits.description: Some ferns used in traditional medicine for respiratory and digestive issues
Ferns increase humidity and improve indoor air quality
Lush foliage creates a calming, biophilic environment
Elegant feathery fronds add texture to shade gardens
Classic indoor plant for Victorian and modern interiors
Essential element in woodland and shade garden designs
Excellent ground cover for shady, moist areas
Perfect for erosion control on shaded slopes

care_tips.title: Provide shade
care_tips.description: Ferns need shade or filtered light. Direct sun burns their delicate fronds.

care_tips.title: Keep soil moist
care_tips.description: Ferns love moisture. Water regularly to keep soil consistently damp but not waterlogged.

care_tips.title: Increase humidity
care_tips.description: Ferns need high humidity. Mist daily or use a humidifier, especially indoors.

care_tips.title: Fertilize lightly
care_tips.description: Feed with half-strength liquid fertilizer monthly during the growing season.

care_tips.title: Remove dead fronds
care_tips.description: Cut brown or dead fronds at the base to keep the plant looking fresh and encourage new growth.

description: Mint is a fast-growing aromatic herb with a unmistakable cool, refreshing flavor. It is one of the most versatile culinary herbs and has been used for millennia in medicine, cooking, and beverages.

benefits.description: Peppermint tea relieves digestive issues and nausea
Menthol in mint acts as a natural decongestant
Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties
Helps relieve tension headaches when applied topically
Mint tea promotes digestion and freshens breath
Increases alertness and mental clarity
Fresh, cool scent used in toothpaste, gum, and body products
Peppermint essential oil for aromatherapy and focus
Quick ground cover for moist, shady areas
Essential herb in Middle Eastern, Mediterranean, and Southeast Asian cuisine
Mint juleps, mojitos, and mint tea are classic beverages
Mint sauce, chutney, and jelly complement lamb dishes

care_tips.title: Grow in containers
care_tips.description: Mint is highly invasive. Always grow in containers or use underground barriers to contain runners.

care_tips.title: Keep soil moist
care_tips.description: Mint thrives in consistently moist soil. It tolerates more water than most herbs.

care_tips.title: Harvest frequently
care_tips.description: Regular harvesting (pinching stems) encourages bushier growth and prevents flowering.

care_tips.title: Cut back in fall
care_tips.description: Cut plants back to ground level in fall. Mint will regrow vigorously in spring.

care_tips.title: Divide every 2-3 years
care_tips.description: Divide overcrowded mint patches to maintain vigor and flavor.

description: Basil is one of the most beloved culinary herbs, with fragrant leaves that are essential to Italian and Southeast Asian cuisines. Its aromatic leaves come in many varieties from sweet to spicy.

benefits.description: Basil contains antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties
Holy basil (Tulsi) is used in Ayurvedic medicine for stress relief
Basil tea helps with digestive issues and nausea
Rich in vitamin K, iron, and calcium
Antibacterial properties support immune health
Beautiful green or purple leaves make attractive garden plants
Aromatic leaves release fragrance when brushed
Essential for pesto, Caprese salad, and Italian cuisine
Holy basil (Tulsi) is used in teas and Southeast Asian cooking
Fresh basil elevates pizzas, pastas, salads, and sauces

care_tips.title: Plant after frost
care_tips.description: Basil is extremely frost-sensitive. Plant outdoors only after all frost danger has passed.

care_tips.title: Pinch regularly
care_tips.description: Pinch off top sets of leaves weekly to encourage bushy growth and prevent flowering.

care_tips.title: Water consistently
care_tips.description: Keep soil moist but not waterlogged. Basil wilts quickly in dry soil.

care_tips.title: Provide full sun
care_tips.description: Basil needs at least 6 hours of direct sun. It grows leggy in shade.

care_tips.title: Harvest from top down
care_tips.description: Always harvest from the top, cutting just above a leaf pair. Never strip leaves from stems.

description: Morning glory is a fast-growing climbing vine with beautiful trumpet-shaped flowers that open in the morning and close by afternoon. It is beloved for its ability to quickly cover fences and trellises with color.

benefits.description: Morning glory seeds have been used in traditional medicine (caution: some species contain alkaloids)
Fast-growing vines provide quick privacy screening
Gardening with morning glories provides daily mindfulness practice
Quick coverage for fences, trellises, and arbors
Hundreds of flowers appear daily during peak bloom
Beautiful blue, purple, pink, and white trumpet flowers
Morning glory leaves are edible and used in some Asian cuisines (water spinach variety)

care_tips.title: Sow seeds directly
care_tips.description: Morning glories don't transplant well. Sow seeds directly in the ground after frost.

care_tips.title: Nick seeds before planting
care_tips.description: File or nick the hard seed coat and soak overnight before planting for better germination.

care_tips.title: Provide a trellis
care_tips.description: Morning glories need a fence, trellis, or strings to climb. They twine counterclockwise.

care_tips.title: Water moderately
care_tips.description: Water regularly but allow soil to dry between waterings. Overwatering reduces blooms.

care_tips.title: Deadhead not needed
care_tips.description: Unlike many annuals, morning glories don't need deadheading. Let them self-sow for next year.
